profile.headline: I build AI platforms that non-technical users can actually use.
profile.lede: Associate Product Manager with 3+ years in B2B SaaS and 5 years in software, currently building AI/ML and Generative AI platform capabilities at Zoho QuickML — turning complex ML and GenAI workflows into products enterprise teams adopt.

about.heading: Product built on evidence, shipped with conviction.
paragraphs: I'm a product manager focused on operationalizing AI and data for enterprise customers. At Zoho QuickML I own the pipeline builder — the drag-and-drop workflow engine that lets non-technical users create end-to-end ML pipelines — and led product definition for Generative AI capabilities including LLM serving, RAG-based knowledge systems, and foundational model integration.

Before product, I spent five years in software engineering at HCL Technologies across network systems, automation, and large-scale data migration. That foundation is why I'm comfortable with API-first platform design and translating deep technical capability into business use cases.
I care about platform thinking, cross-functional execution, and the unglamorous adoption work that decides whether a product survives.

contact.heading: Let's talk product.
contact.text: Open to Product Manager and Technical Product Manager roles in teams building AI platforms, developer platforms, and intelligent SaaS.

blogs.title: Miller's Law
blogs.blurb: The working memory of the average person can retain approximately 7 (+- 2) items at a given time

blogs.title: Hick's Law
blogs.blurb: The time taken to make a decision increases with the complexity and the number of options.

projects.name: PM Navigator
projects.blurb: An AI multi-agent system that reads the live PM job market, finds your skill gaps against standard competency frameworks, and generates a 30/60/90-day plan. Built locally on Ollama (qwen2.5:14b) with a ChromaDB knowledge base and reflexion-pattern critique agents.

projects.name: Food Detector App
projects.blurb: A food-image classifier for 101 items with confidence scores, built on EfficientNetV2B0 transfer learning, a custom preprocessing pipeline, and a feedback-driven dataset improvement loop.

articles.title: 🚀 Building an AI-powered Food Classification System 🍔🍕🥗– Journey So Far!
articles.blurb: Fully functioning food recognition app that can classify 101 different food items with the basic capability to upload & predict, and generate proper responses with confidence scores for further analysis.

projects.name: HARDCOPY
projects.blurb: Founded a peer-to-peer book lending/borrowing platform. Ran user research (55+ survey responses), led a 5-person cross-functional team, scoped ~100 low-fi wireframes, and built the MVP roadmap.
